title: Profile Settings tab with canister-backed update_profile and optimistic saves

requirements.summary: Implement a functional Profile Settings form in the Settings page Profile tab (principal read-only, display name, timezone, notification toggles, save).
acceptanceCriteria: In SettingsRouteScreen.tsx, the TabsContent for value="profile" renders a form UI instead of the current placeholder text.
The Principal ID is shown as a read-only value (not editable) and uses the authenticated Internet Identity principal.
The Display name field is editable and is prefilled from the current stored profile when available.
A Timezone selector is present and shows the currently saved timezone when available (otherwise a sensible default is selected).
At least two notification preference toggles are shown, with clear English labels, and reflect saved state when available.
A Save button is present and is disabled while saving.

file_operations.description: Create a Profile Settings form component composed from existing shadcn/ui primitives (do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui). The form must: (1) display the authenticated principal (read-only) using the Internet Identity context, (2) allow editing display name, (3) provide a timezone selector with a sensible default, (4) render at least two notification preference toggles with clear English labels, and (5) provide a Save button that disables while saving and triggers a canister-backed save flow via the Profile Settings mutation hook. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Replace only the Profile tab placeholder content by rendering the new ProfileSettingsForm component inside TabsContent value="profile". Keep the Connections and Subscription tabs’ content/behavior unchanged. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add React Query query + optimistic mutation for loading/saving profile settings via update_profile with rollback on error.
acceptanceCriteria: A React Query query exists to load the current user’s profile settings (display name, timezone, notification preferences) from the canister and populate the form.
A React Query mutation exists to save profile settings via the canister’s update_profile method.
The mutation performs an optimistic update to the relevant React Query cache key(s) before the canister call resolves.
If the canister call fails, the optimistic update is rolled back to the previous cached value(s).
On success, the saved settings are reflected in the UI and the relevant queries are invalidated/refetched to ensure consistency.

file_operations.description: Create dedicated React Query hooks for Profile Settings: a query hook that loads the caller profile settings from the canister (reusing the existing ['currentUserProfile'] cache key strategy), and a mutation hook that calls the canister's update_profile and performs optimistic cache updates with rollback on error and invalidation/refetch on success. Ensure the mutation exposes isPending to support disabling the Save button. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire the form to the React Query hooks: initialize form state from the loaded profile, keep inputs controlled, and on Save trigger the optimistic mutation calling update_profile. On error, display a user-friendly message (e.g., via the existing ToastProvider/useToast and normalizeActorError utility) and ensure UI reflects rolled-back state. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Align frontend type definitions used by the Profile Settings form with the updated canister interface so update_profile and the profile shape used by the form compile cleanly (e.g., timezone + notification preference fields). Keep changes limited to type/interface alignment required for the Profile Settings feature.

requirements.summary: Ensure Connections and Subscription tabs remain unchanged while implementing Profile tab.
acceptanceCriteria: TabsContent for value="connections" remains functionally unchanged.
TabsContent for value="subscription" remains functionally unchanged (including admin-only visibility logic).
Only the Profile tab content is expanded/implemented.

file_operations.description: Confirm the implementation confines edits to the Profile tab region only: do not alter the Connections tab content, do not alter the Subscription tab content or its admin-only visibility/auto-correct logic, and keep tab triggers/values intact.
